support.title: A small token to keep 1 Darjeeling running
support.amount_line: ₹12 for the year — a one-time platform support & convenience fee that goes toward maintenance, hosting and building costs.
support.body: That's ₹1 a month. It keeps the app running for travellers and keeps listings free for the small homestays, drivers and cafés on it.
support.reassurance: Valid for one year. Nothing auto-charges — we'll ask you again next year.
support.skip: Not now — browse without an account
support.error: Payment could not be completed. Please try again.

donate.title: Donate for good
donate.lead: Give what you like — every rupee goes into keeping 1 Darjeeling running and its listings free for small local businesses.
donate.note: A one-off gift. It buys nothing and unlocks nothing — it just helps.
donate.error: Payment could not be completed. Please try again.

responsible.title: Responsible Tourism
responsible.lead: Darjeeling is a fragile Himalayan ecosystem. Let's protect it together.

principles.d: Carry reusable bottles, bags and cups. Refill at local shops.
Choose homestays over chains. Your money reaches local families.
Do not feed or chase wild animals. Observe from a distance.
Walk marked trails only. Don't trample undergrowth.
Ask permission before photographing monasteries and people.
Keep noise low — the hills and their creatures prefer it that way.

sections.p: Name, phone, email and booking details — only to deliver our service.
To process bookings, connect you with providers, and improve the platform.
You may request deletion or export of your data at any time.

about.title: Built in Darjeeling, for Darjeeling.
about.lead: The hills already have everything a traveller needs. 1 Darjeeling simply puts it in one place, and makes sure the people who live here are the ones who benefit.
about.mission_body:
Visitors arrive in Darjeeling and end up booking through agents who have never walked these ridges. The homestay owner, the driver who knows every switchback, the shop weaving shawls upstairs, the cafe that has served the same breakfast since 1911 - they are hard to find online, and harder to book.

We put them on one map. No middlemen deciding who gets seen, no commission quietly taken out of a family's earnings.

pillars.d: Every listing belongs to someone who lives and works in Darjeeling. Providers write their own descriptions, set their own prices and keep what they earn.
Homestays, drivers, tea houses, cafes, festivals and Himalayan wildlife - all in one app, instead of scattered across a dozen sites and phone numbers.
The entire site works in English, Nepali, Hindi and Bengali, so a provider in Ghum can list a room as easily as a visitor from Kolkata can book it.
The price a provider sets is the price you see. Nothing is inflated to cover a commission you were never told about.

steps.d: Browse stays, drivers, shops, cafes, festivals and biodiversity across Darjeeling and the hills around it.
Reserve a homestay or a driver online. For shops, cafes and events, get directions and go.
Follow the community pledge - the hills are a fragile ecosystem, and they stay beautiful only if visitors help.

about.providers_body: If you host guests, drive visitors, pour tea or make things by hand, your work belongs here. Listing takes a few minutes and puts you in front of every visitor using the app.
about.team_lead: 1 Darjeeling is designed and built by a small team at Studio 1947.
about.team_note: Built in the hills, for the hills.
